deck_pages.title: CONFIDENTIAL 6
deck_pages.text:
CONFIDENTIAL 6
FINANCIAL SUMMARY
QUARTERLY PERFORMANCE UPDATE – 04/20/26
Headspace reported 4Q25 bookings of $89.1MM and revenue of $53.6MM. The bookings performance was flat on a year-over-year basis and was an 10% miss versus the Company’s $99.3MM 4Q25 budget,
while the revenue performance represents a 6% year-over-year decline and a 8% miss versus the Company’s $58.1MM budget. The top-line underperformance was a result of (a) the Company’s dependency
on a small number of high-ACV contracts with long and unpredictable sales cycles, (b) lower product utilization by health plans; (c) softer new business performance, upsale delays and higher churn with
Employer and Partnership customers; and (d) slower than expected growth in the Company’s DTC therapy offering. Headspace reported Gross Margin of 74.6% in 4Q25, which was an outperformance versus
budget, and the Company held EBITDA at breakeven levels. The Company underperformed its FY25 revenue target by 5%, reporting $216.3MM of revenue versus a $237.6MM budget, but an in-line
performance at the Gross Margin line (73.6% versus a 73.0% target) combined with continued cost-actions led to EBITDA of $10.9MM versus a $6.7MM budget.
The Company has made substantial leadership changes over the past 15 months under new-CEO Tom Pickett. Recent additions to the Company’s executive leadership team include Lisa Gross (Chief People
Officer); Nihal Shah (Chief Financial Officer), Fay Kallel (Chief Product Officer) and Charles Fan (Chief Commercial Officer). Pickett has now replaced seven of his ten-member executive team since his arrival 15
months ago. Pickett has also driven Headspace towards sustained profitability and has led the development of a coherent strategy to capture the industrial logic of the combination of Headspace and Ginger.
Despite the cost actions and the personnel changes, Headspace continues to struggle with (a) re-igniting revenue growth – the Company’s growth areas are not overcoming the challenges presented by churn
in its consumer business and both low utilization and churn in its enterprise business, and (b) supporting the increasing cost of its debt load – the cost of interest on the Company’s $77MM debt balance is
now consuming much of the EBITDA created by the cost actions that the Company has taken over the past 24 months. Revenue has declined every year since FY23 – falling from $236.3MM in FY23 to a
forecasted $201.2MM in FY26. Meanwhile, interest payments will consume almost all of the $10.9MM of EBITDA that the Company generated in FY25. Management has attempted to restructure the debt, but
in the absence of any interest from existing owners to invest additional equity in the Company, the lender (Oxford Finance) has demanded that the Company explore a sale (and the Company’s board formed a
special committee and engaged an advisor to seek potential buyers for the business). With the turnaround incomplete, Pickett expects interest to be muted and for bids to value the business at less than
$500MM (inclusive of $40MM of net debt), which could represent less than our invested capital. However, Pickett believes that a sale of the business or a combination with a competitor will best position
Headspace for success and is committed to this path. We have begun to explore our options (including both receiving cash proceeds in a sale and “rolling” our equity into a combination that has the potential
to drive attractive long-term returns). We have marked down our carrying value from a 2.1x MOIC to a 0.6x MOIC to reflect this $500MM TEV and will continue to share our thoughts as the situation evolves.
HEADSPACE
Headspace operates the world’s most accessible and comprehensive digital mental health and wellness platform, dramatically improving access to a full
spectrum of affordable care. The Company was formed by the 2021 merger of Headspace and Ginger, combining Headspace’s leading DTC wellbeing
platform and brand with Ginger’s platform for providing coaching, cogitative behavioral therapy (CBT), and higher-acuity clinical interventions. Headspace
delivers the most comprehensive mental health offering on the market and is uniquely positioned to capture increasing demand for mental health solutions
by both consumers and enterprise customers.
2026

deck_pages.title: CONFIDENTIAL 18
deck_pages.text:
CONFIDENTIAL 18
Advance/Newhouse Investment Partnership – Portfolio Summary Notes
Notes to Portfolio Summary
1. Ownership metrics as of March 31, 2024, except where noted differently for subsequent transactions. All other valuation, capi tal structure, and AVP investment metrics based on most recent equity financing transaction, except
where noted differently for subsequent transactions.
2. Last Post-Money Valuation defined as the equity value of the most recent preferred financing round or other transaction establis hing a value for the company.
3. Current Holding Value defined as 1) the Last Post -Money Valuation when the AVP investment is held at the value of the most recent preferred financing round or 2) the implied valuation of the company calculated as the Unrealized
Value divided by the Ownership % for an AVP investment determined by AVP to be held at a different MOIC.
4. Total Liquidation Preference defined as the total preferred liquidation preference plus any debt securities outstanding.
5. Closed $30MM Series D financing at a $210MM pre -money valuation in 2Q21. Advance did not participate in the round and owns 1.6% on a fully -diluted basis including Series B conversion adjustment in FY17 from settlement
between Series B Preferred shareholders and the Company. Held at 0.5x MOIC based on Company performance and outlook.
6. Closed $120MM acquisition of Tradesy in April 2022 with a combination of cash and equity consideration. To finance the transaction, Vestiaire raised €81MM of new ca pital from existing investors at the same price as the October
2021 Series H led by Softbank. Advance invested €1.8MM in the acquisition financing, bringing total Advace invested capital to €31.0MM for 7.0% fully -diluted ownership. Values based on USD/EUR exchange rate as of September 30,
2022. Metrics do not reflect the actively contemplated follow -on financing led by insiders. Advance is expected to invest €2.5MM with a close date in September 2023."
7. Completed merger with SPAC vehicle and began trading on the Nasdaq on October 31, 2021. Advance owns 1.3MM shares of common s tock. Unrealized value based on closing share price as of April 3, 2025. Advance owns 0.4%, and
ownership % reflects outstanding shares as of 10 -K dated December 31, 2024.
8. Priced IPO at $21 per share and began trading on NASDAQ on October 26, 2021. The Company sold 17MM shares to raise $357MM of primary capital. After a 1:20 reverse stock split at RTR, AVP owns 22K shares of common stock and
Advance owns 84K for total ownership of 106K shares. Unrealized Value based on closing share price as of April 3, 2025. Advance and AVP owns 2.9% in aggregate, and ownership % reflects outstanding shares as of April 3, 2025.
9. Priced IPO at $18 per share and began trading on the NYSE on April 1, 2021. The Company sold 25MM shares to raise $450MM of prim ary capital. Advance sold its remaining 5.0MM shares of Class A common stock at an average
price of $10.48 per share, generating $52.3MM in proceeds. This brings total proceeds generated over the life of the investme nt to $76.7MM and represents a fully-realized 5.6x multiple of ANIP ‘s $13.7MM investment in Compass.
10. Wrote-off investment due to lack of consistent quarterly reporting by Company. The Company was not able to realize an asset sale originally contemplated.
11. Sold to Digimarc in January 2022 for $100MM of total consideration split into payments across two tranches. The first tranche of $50MM of Digimarc stock (less holdbacks) was delivered to shareholders at closing. A second tranche
of $50MM of Digimarc stock would have been delivered in September 2022 based achieving forecasted ARR goals as of February 20 22; however, Digimarc notified investors in March 2022 that these goals where not met and there
would be no additional payout to investors in the second tranche given the magnitude of underperformance relative to forecast . Advance received 9,700 Digimarc shares in the first tranche and sold these shares on February 8, 2022
at an average price of $29.024 for total net proceeds of $281K.
12. Fully realized investment as of April 2018 with total proceeds of $391.3MM. Advance sold its remaining 4.7% fully -diluted stake in the Company’s public stock in April 2018, realizing $383MM of gross proceeds in addition to the
$8.3MM of secondary sale proceeds previously realized over the investment period.
13. Sold to Snap for $7.5MM in 4Q16. Advance received $166K of cash consideration at closing, $166K from holdback in April 2018, and $143K in September 2018 due to escrow adjustments being better than anticipated. Total proceeds
to Advance were $476K.
14. Sold to SAP for $350MM in 4Q17. Advance received $8.6MM of cash consideration at close and an additional $1.5MM from escrow p aid in March 2019 for total proceeds of $10.1MM.
15. Sold to Mastercard for $75MM in 3Q20. Advance received $800K of cash consideration at closing.
16. Wrote-off investment due to lack of consistent quarterly reporting by Company.
17. Sold to Optimizely for $121.5MM in December 2021. Advance received $700K of cash consideration at closing.
18. Entered into a pre-packaged bankruptcy with investing. Given the Company's history, size of Advance's stake, and that the last i nvestment was made in 2014, we declined to participate. The new capital structure will cram down our
equity, and we do not expect to see a return on investment.
19. Monoqi's insolvency administrator arranged a debt -free asset sale to Lumaland Group, a publicly-traded German retailer for €3.0MM. Advance and other shareholders did not receive any consideration for the s ale.
20. Sold 38,415 shares of Rubicon Project common stock of 06/19/19 at $5.6544 per share for net proceeds of $216,441. Shares were received from acquisition of iSocket in 4Q14.
21. iHeartMedia purchased Advance's ownership in Unified for $1.5MM, resulting in a return on invested capital of 0.1x. Given the rapid deterioration of the Company's financials, aggressive stance taken by the Company's lenders to this
deterioration, and the lack of strategic interest after an extended effort to sell the Company, it was determined that this was the most attractive offer Advance would receive for its Unified shares.
22. Sold to AcuityAds for $10.5MM in 1Q17. All cash consideration was used to settle outstanding financial liabilities and debts of the Company.
